# API Contracts Checklist: PRSG-008 Layer Planner

**Purpose**: Validate that the planner JSON contract is complete, stable, fixture-testable, and directly consumable by PRSG-009.
**Created**: 2026-06-10
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)
**Domain**: api-contracts · **Audience**: PR reviewer · **Depth**: formal release gate

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all top-level envelope fields required for every planner outcome? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014a, Contract §Envelope]
- [ ] CHK002 Are planner statuses and exit-code mappings closed and stable for callers? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014b, Spec §FR-015, Contract §Exit Codes]
- [ ] CHK003 Are successful plan payload fields complete enough for PRSG-009 to consume ordered increments without reading `tasks.md` prose? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014c, Spec §FR-014d]
- [x] CHK004 Are diagnostic codes defined for usage and input-error outcomes as well as invalid-plan and warning outcomes? [Resolved, Spec §FR-014k, Contract §Diagnostics]
- [ ] CHK005 Are warning diagnostics included in the same machine-readable envelope as successful plans? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-014e]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Are increment object fields defined with stable names, value meanings, and ordering semantics? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014c, Data Model §Increment]
- [ ] CHK007 Are task object fields defined with stable names and checkbox-derived value meanings? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014d, Data Model §Task]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the supported task status enum explicitly limited to `todo` and `done`? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014d, Schema §task.status]
- [x] CHK009 Are semantic increment IDs constrained precisely enough for downstream consumers instead of left as arbitrary strings? [Resolved, Spec §FR-010a, Schema §semantic_increment_id]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the advisory-size object limited to counts-only fields and explicitly separated from PRSG-006 reviewability gates? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014f, Contract §Increment Object]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Do the spec, contract document, schema, and data model agree on the same top-level envelope fields? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014a, Contract §Envelope, Schema §required]
- [ ] CHK012 Do the spec, contract document, schema, and data model agree on the same status enum values? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014b, Contract §Exit Codes, Schema §status]
- [ ] CHK013 Do the invalid-plan diagnostic code lists match across spec, data model, and schema? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014g, Data Model §Diagnostic, Schema §diagnostic.code]
- [ ] CHK014 Do warning diagnostic code lists match across spec, data model, and schema? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014h, Data Model §Diagnostic, Schema §diagnostic.code]
- [x] CHK015 Are diagnostic `code` and `severity` values constrained consistently so warning-only codes cannot be emitted as errors and error-only codes cannot be emitted as warnings? [Resolved, Spec §FR-014m, Schema §warningDiagnostic/errorDiagnostic]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK016 Can valid, warning, invalid-plan, and usage/input fixtures objectively assert the same envelope shape? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-020, Spec §SC-003, Spec §SC-004, Spec §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK017 Can fixture tests assert deterministic output without ignoring runtime-dependent fields? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK018 Can malformed-plan fixtures assert structured JSON errors and concise stderr summaries separately? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-003, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK019 Can usage/input fixtures assert exit `2`, structured JSON output, and no repository writes? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-004, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-015]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK020 Are successful plan, warning, invalid-plan, and input-error API scenarios all covered by requirements? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 3]
- [ ] CHK021 Are autopilot handoff requirements clear about when a layer plan is required and when it is skipped? [Coverage, Spec §FR-017, Spec §FR-017a]
- [ ] CHK022 Are planner non-goals clear enough to prevent PRSG-009 branch, PR body, or restack topology from leaking into this API? [Coverage, Spec §FR-019, Reviewability Budget]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK023 Are all invalid-plan edge cases mapped to stable diagnostic codes? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-014g]
- [ ] CHK024 Are missing file/test references modeled as warnings with enough detail for callers to distinguish file versus test references? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014h]
- [x] CHK025 Are per-code diagnostic `details` payloads specified enough for fixture tests and downstream consumers to avoid parsing human messages? [Resolved, Spec §FR-014l, Contract §Diagnostics, Schema §diagnostic.details]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK026 Is PRSG-009 explicitly named as the downstream consumer while PRSG-008 remains independent from PRSG-009 emission behavior? [Dependency, Spec §FR-019, Spec §Assumptions]

## Re-run Verification

- [ ] CHK027 Are input-error diagnostic codes now present in spec, contract, and schema so exit `2` outcomes are machine-readable? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014k, Contract §Diagnostics, Schema §diagnostic.code]
- [ ] CHK028 Is the semantic increment ID grammar now constrained in spec, data model, contract, and schema? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010a, Data Model §Increment, Schema §semantic_increment_id]
- [ ] CHK029 Does the schema now prevent warning-only codes from appearing in `errors[]` and error-only codes from appearing in `warnings[]`? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014m, Schema §warningDiagnostic/errorDiagnostic]
- [ ] CHK030 Are per-code diagnostic `details` payloads now closed and fixture-testable without parsing `message` text? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014l, Contract §Diagnostics, Schema §diagnostic.details]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: JSON contract fields and enum stability; machine-readable error shape; PRSG-009 consumption without task-prose parsing.
- Defaults applied from checklist workflow: reviewer audience, formal release-gate depth.
- Remediation: four API-contract gaps were resolved in `spec.md`, `data-model.md`, and the `plan-layers` contract/schema artifacts.
